title: Spring Claw Maintenance: Preparing Your Kits for Warmer Weather
description: A comprehensive guide to transitioning your claw kits from winter to spring care routines in the northern hemisphere.
main_content: As the northern hemisphere enters May, it's essential to adjust your claw kit maintenance for the changing season. Spring brings increased humidity and temperature fluctuations that can affect claw health. Start by inspecting all kits for winter wear—look for cracks, splits, or brittleness caused by dry indoor heating. Gradually introduce higher humidity environments to prevent shock. For outdoor kits, apply a protective balm with UV blockers to shield against stronger spring sunlight. Trim any damaged tips using sterilized shears. Finally, transition from heavy winter oils to lighter, breathable moisturizers that won't attract dirt and pollen. Remember to clean and dry kits thoroughly after each use to prevent fungal growth in warmer conditions.
key_takeaways: Inspect claw kits for winter damage before transitioning care
Gradually increase humidity to avoid shock to kits
Use UV-protective balm for outdoor kits
Switch from heavy oils to light moisturizers for spring
Maintain cleanliness to prevent fungal issues
